title: Recover From AGV Fault During Exchange
summary: Restore the exchange location after a lifted AGV faults while leaving the RMS by stopping the RMS, removing the affected AGV, checking whether the trailing AGV back fills, removing the trailing AGV if it does not back fill, and restoring the location with a new tote and an empty AGV in the zone.
when_to_use: Use when an AGV faults during exchange and the AGV is lifted and faulted while leaving the RMS, as described in manual section 5.8.2.5.
not_for: Do not use for AGV bump fault recovery scenarios covered elsewhere in the manual.
Do not use for faulted AGV with tote recovery scenarios covered elsewhere in the manual.
Do not use when detailed tote introduction or empty AGV placement instructions are required, because this source does not provide those steps.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Execute RMS stop, identify affected AGV(s), remove AGV(s) using AGV API Controls, verify back-fill condition, and restore the affected location with a new tote and empty AGV as supported by the source.

safety_notes: The procedure begins with E-stop of the RMS.
This is not marked support-safe in the candidate and involves system-level AGV removal from RMS.
The source does not provide detailed recovery actions if AGV removal cannot be completed.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the RMS E-stop control
Access to AGV API Controls referenced on page 24
Ability to identify the faulted lifted AGV and the trailing AGV

system_preconditions: An AGV fault during exchange has occurred.
The affected AGV is lifted and faulted while leaving the RMS.

visual_references.description: AGV API Controls screen used for AGV removal and related AGV commands.
System screen showing shut-down mode, useful when RMS is E-stopped.
Nearby fault recovery screen showing a faulted AGV with tote; useful as contextual AGV fault reference.
Related recovery screen for adding an AGV to a zone; useful as contextual reference for zone restoration.
Add Tote screen reference from sorter scanner operation; useful as contextual reference for introducing a tote.

steps.title: E-stop the RMS
steps.purpose: Place the system into a stopped state before AGV fault recovery actions are performed.
steps.expected_result: The RMS is stopped and the recovery can proceed under the documented fault-recovery condition.
steps.healthy_condition: RMS is in a stopped or shut-down state suitable for recovery actions.
steps.failure_condition: RMS does not stop or cannot be confirmed as stopped.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System shut-down mode indication after E-stop.

stop_or_escalate_if: System stop state cannot be confirmed.

steps.title: Identify the faulted lifted AGV
steps.purpose: Determine which AGV faulted during exchange so the correct AGV is removed from RMS.
steps.instruction: Identify the AGV that is lifted and has faulted while leaving the RMS during the exchange.
steps.expected_result: The specific lifted AGV that faulted while leaving the RMS is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: The affected AGV can be clearly identified as the lifted AGV faulted while leaving.
steps.failure_condition: The affected AGV cannot be confidently identified.

actions.label: Identify faulted AGV
actions.exact_action: Identify the AGV that is lifted that has faulted out while leaving from the RMS.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Faulted AGV visual context to help recognize the affected AGV condition.

stop_or_escalate_if: The faulted lifted AGV cannot be identified.
Multiple AGVs appear affected and the source does not provide disambiguation steps.

steps.title: Remove the faulted AGV from RMS
steps.purpose: Remove the identified faulted AGV from the RMS using the documented AGV control interface.
steps.instruction: Remove the identified lifted AGV that faulted while leaving from the RMS using AGV API Controls referenced on page 24.
steps.expected_result: The faulted AGV is removed from the RMS.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is successfully removed using AGV API Controls.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be removed from the RMS or removal status cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Remove faulted AGV in AGV API Controls
actions.exact_action: Remove the AGV that is lifted that has faulted out while leaving from the RMS. (See "AGV API Controls" on page 24.)

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: AGV API Controls interface, especially Remove AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: AGV API Controls are not accessible.
The AGV cannot be removed through AGV API Controls.

steps.title: Check whether the trailing AGV back fills
steps.purpose: Determine whether the AGV behind the removed faulted AGV automatically back fills the location.
steps.instruction: Check whether the AGV behind the faulted AGV back fills.
steps.expected_result: A determination is made whether the trailing AGV back fills.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV behind the removed AGV back fills the position.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV behind does not back fill.

actions.label: Verify back-fill behavior
actions.exact_action: If the AGV behind it does not back fill, also remove that AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: Unexpected AGV behavior occurs and the source provides no further guidance.

steps.title: Remove the trailing AGV if it does not back fill
steps.purpose: Clear the trailing AGV from RMS when it fails to back fill after removal of the faulted AGV.
steps.instruction: If the AGV behind the faulted AGV does not back fill, remove that AGV as well using AGV API Controls referenced on page 24.
steps.expected_result: Any trailing AGV that does not back fill is removed from the RMS.
steps.healthy_condition: The non-back-filling trailing AGV is removed from RMS.
steps.failure_condition: The trailing AGV cannot be removed or removal cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Remove non-back-filling trailing AGV
actions.exact_action: If the AGV behind it does not back fill, also remove that AGV.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: AGV API Controls interface used to remove the trailing AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: The trailing AGV does not back fill and cannot be removed.
The source does not provide next actions after failed removal.

steps.title: Introduce a new tote at the affected location
steps.purpose: Restore tote presence at the affected exchange location.
steps.instruction: Introduce a new tote at that location.
steps.expected_result: A new tote is introduced at the affected location.
steps.healthy_condition: The affected location has a replacement tote.
steps.failure_condition: A new tote cannot be introduced or the source-required method is not available.

actions.label: Introduce replacement tote
actions.exact_action: Introduce a new tote at that location.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Add Tote screen context for tote introduction, if used by local process.

stop_or_escalate_if: A new tote is not available.
The source does not provide the detailed tote introduction method needed for execution.

steps.title: Place an empty AGV in the affected zone
steps.purpose: Restore an empty AGV to the affected zone after the exchange fault recovery.
steps.instruction: Place an empty AGV in that zone.
steps.expected_result: An empty AGV is placed in the affected zone.
steps.healthy_condition: The affected zone contains the required empty AGV.
steps.failure_condition: An empty AGV cannot be placed in the zone or placement cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Place empty AGV in zone
actions.exact_action: Place an empty AGV in that zone.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Related add-AGV-to-zone screen for contextual guidance on restoring AGV presence in a zone.

stop_or_escalate_if: An empty AGV is not available.
The source does not provide the detailed method for placing the empty AGV in the zone.

success_criteria: The faulted lifted AGV is removed from the RMS.
If the trailing AGV does not back fill, it is also removed from the RMS.
A new tote is introduced at the affected location.
An empty AGV is placed in the affected zone.
healthy_conditions: RMS is successfully E-stopped before recovery actions.
The faulted lifted AGV is correctly identified.
AGV removal through AGV API Controls completes successfully.
The trailing AGV either back fills or is removed if it does not.
The affected location is repopulated with a tote and an empty AGV.
failure_conditions: The faulted AGV cannot be identified.
The faulted AGV cannot be removed through AGV API Controls.
The trailing AGV does not back fill and cannot be removed.
The source does not provide enough detail to complete tote introduction or empty AGV placement.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if AGV removal through AGV API Controls cannot be completed.
Escalate if the faulted AGV or trailing AGV cannot be confidently identified.
Escalate if detailed instructions are needed for introducing the new tote or placing the empty AGV, because the source does not provide them.
Use a higher-support role if local policy requires additional authority for system-level AGV removal.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Remove AGV control and related AGV API functions.
Related zone restoration screen for adding AGV presence.

missing_details: The source does not provide detailed instructions for how to identify the faulted AGV in the interface.
The source does not provide step-by-step AGV API Controls interaction details for removal.
The source does not provide detailed instructions for how to introduce the new tote at the affected location.
The source does not provide detailed instructions for how to place the empty AGV in the affected zone.
The source does not describe what to do if AGV removal through AGV API Controls cannot be completed.
The source does not provide explicit completion confirmations, timing, or validation checks after restoration.
